Fixes handling of native / alien widgets when (un)docking (#820)

* Initial plan

* Fix drawing artifacts when content widgets call winId()

When a content widget (e.g. VTK/OpenGL) calls winId() to get a native
window handle, Qt propagates native-window creation up the ancestor chain.
This makes CDockWidget, CDockAreaWidget, and CDockContainerWidget all
become native OS windows unexpectedly. Reparenting these native dock
widgets during floating or resizing then causes drawing artifacts.

Fix: set Qt::WA_DontCreateNativeAncestors on the content widget in
CDockWidget::setWidget() to stop native-window creation from propagating
up into the dock hierarchy.

Co-authored-by: RubendeBruin <34062862+RubendeBruin@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix docking system to handle mix of native and alien Qt widgets

Three targeted fixes for the native/alien widget mixing problem:

1. Revert WA_DontCreateNativeAncestors from CDockWidget::setWidget().
   Setting it on the content widget created a broken native-inside-alien
   hierarchy: the QScrollArea (alien) could not clip its native child, so
   content escaped the scrollbox bounds; and alien CAutoHideDockContainer
   could not appear above the native content widget.
   Allowing natural propagation means QScrollArea also goes native, and OS
   parent/child window clipping works correctly.

2. Fix CDockContainerWidget::removeDockArea to use internalWinId() instead
   of testAttribute(WA_NativeWindow). WA_NativeWindow is only set when a
   widget is explicitly made native; widgets that became native through
   upward propagation from a descendant calling winId() have a real native
   window handle (internalWinId() != 0) but WA_NativeWindow may be unset.
   Setting their parent to nullptr would create a free-floating invisible
   OS window and cause drawing artifacts.

3. Fix CAutoHideDockContainer::collapseView: when the parent dock container
   is native but the auto-hide panel is still alien, promote the panel to a
   native window (winId()) before raise(). The OS always renders native
   child windows above a parent window's painted alien content, so an alien
   auto-hide panel can never appear above native sibling dock areas. Once
   the panel has its own OS-level window, raise() operates on OS Z-order
   and correctly places it above native content siblings.

Co-authored-by: RubendeBruin <34062862+RubendeBruin@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: guarantee DB connection close on exception (database is locked bug)

Every database function now wraps its work in try/finally so conn.close()
is always called — even when an exception is raised mid-operation (e.g. a
UNIQUE constraint violation on duplicate device name). Previously, any
uncaught exception would leak the connection, leaving SQLite's write lock
held until the process restarted, causing subsequent saves to fail with
'database is locked'.

Fix RPC starvation on 1-CPU VPS during block sync

Root cause: tokio defaults to num_cpus worker threads (1 on VPS).
Synchronous sled I/O during block processing monopolizes the only
worker thread, starving RPC, timers, and network I/O indefinitely.

Three fixes:
- Force worker_threads=4 in tokio::main regardless of CPU count
- Replace yield_now() with sleep(1ms) between block batches so the
  I/O driver gets polled (yield_now re-queues without polling I/O)
- Add flush_storage_async() using spawn_blocking for fsync calls
  so they run on the blocking thread pool, not tokio workers

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Make hooks the ONLY DB writer — remove agent manual INSERT

Root cause: agents manually INSERT into tasks table with guessed
project_id, causing misattribution. Hooks have a deterministic
registration gate (cwd → folder match) but agents don't.

Fix:
- Removed 'insert a row into tasks table' from copilot-instructions.md
  and CLAUDE.md. Agents never touch progress.db directly.
- Rewrote progress-logger.sh to read Copilot CLI session state:
  workspace.yaml (summary) + checkpoints (overview, takeaways, gotchas)
  instead of just file diffs.
- Falls back to activity log file changes if no session state found.
- Skips index.md when finding latest checkpoint.

The hook pipeline is now the single deterministic write path:
  sessionEnd → match cwd to project → read session state → INSERT

commit.message:
doc: add node-requirements.md -- formal node firmware requirements (#11)

* doc: add node-requirements.md -- formal node firmware requirements

New file docs/node-requirements.md with 33 requirements (ND-0100
through ND-0702) covering:

- Protocol and communication (frame format, CBOR encoding, frame size)
- Wake cycle (WAKE message, COMMAND processing, sleep interval)
- Authentication and replay protection (HMAC, response binding,
  sequence numbers, nonce generation)
- Key storage and provisioning (PSK flash partition, USB pairing,
  factory reset, secure boot)
- Program transfer and execution (chunked transfer, hash verification,
  A/B partitions, ephemeral storage, post-update execution)
- BPF environment (helper API stability, bus/communication/map/system
  helpers, execution constraints, map memory budget)
- Timing and retries (WAKE retry, chunk retry, response timeout)

Also adds node-requirements.md to README Further Reading section.

commit.message:
feat: ShardCore, StateRegistry, message enums, and mutation logic (#3)

* feat: ShardCore, StateRegistry, message enums, and mutation logic

PR 2 of the dev plan — implements the pure state machine core:

- ShardCore<S, V>: pure state machine with ArcSwap view map
  - apply_mutation / apply_mutation_with_delta
  - accept_inbound_snapshot / accept_inbound_delta
  - should_accept (incarnation, age) ordering
  - stale_peers, mark_stale, on_node_joined, on_node_left
- StateRegistry: type-erased shard storage with AnyStateShard trait
  - register, lookup, lookup_typed, broadcast_node_joined/left
- Message enums: SimpleShardMsg, DeltaShardMsg, SyncEngineMsg, ChangeFeedMsg
- InboundSnapshot struct for clean parameter passing

Tests: 43 ShardCore unit tests + 9 registry integration tests
Coverage: TEST-05..07, PVM-01..03, SHARD-01..08,10, MUT-01..06, DMUT-01..06,
         REG-01..07, REG-04a, plus additional edge cases

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* perf: replace monolithic ArcSwap view map with per-node ArcSwap

Introduces ViewMap<V> — a two-level ArcSwap data structure that avoids
cloning the entire HashMap on every single-node update:

  ArcSwap<HashMap<NodeId, Arc<ArcSwap<StateViewObject<V>>>>>
    outer (rare: node join/leave)    inner (frequent: O(1) atomic swap)

Performance characteristics:
- Read single node: 2 atomic loads, fully lock-free
- Update single node: 1 atomic store, no map clone (was O(n) deep clone)
- Node join/leave: clone outer map (cheap Arc pointer bumps), rare
- Snapshot all: O(n) — unchanged, but now only needed for bulk reads

ShardCore no longer exposes the raw view_map. New APIs:
- get_view(NodeId) -> Option<Arc<StateViewObject<V>>>
- snapshot() -> HashMap<NodeId, StateViewObject<V>>
- view_count() -> usize

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: introduce StateVersion struct, simplify delta API

- Add StateVersion { incarnation, age } with derived Ord (lexicographic)
  replacing scattered (incarnation, age) pairs throughout the codebase
- StateObject/StateViewObject now use version: StateVersion instead of
  separate age/incarnation fields
- Merge pending_remote_age + pending_remote_incarnation into single
  pending_remote_version: Option<StateVersion>
- Remove to_age from accept_inbound_delta — delta always advances by 1
- Remove ShardCore::should_accept() — replaced by StateVersion::cmp()
- Simplify InboundSnapshot, MutationOutcome, DeltaMutationOutcome to
  carry version: StateVersion instead of separate fields

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: rename StateVersion to Generation

Avoids confusion with wire_version and storage_version fields.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: rename version field to generation

Renames the 'version' field in StateObject, StateViewObject, and
InboundSnapshot to 'generation' to avoid any confusion with
wire_version and storage_version fields. Also renames
pending_remote_version to pending_remote_generation.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: use Generation throughout all message enums and simplify delta API

- Replace incarnation/age fields with Generation in shard_msg, sync_msg, feed_msg, sync_message
- Remove from_age/to_age from delta messages; use single generation field
- Remove min_required_age from RequestSnapshot (YAGNI)

* docs: update design docs for Generation, ViewMap, and simplified delta API

  load-then-store is safe with single writer)

  node join placeholder marked stale
